rulings.Case Title: EUGENE BARNES VS. EDWARD MILLER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 4, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANTS VINH CAO AND MAI CAO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to April 22, 2022 per stipulation and order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SUTRO PROPERTY MANAGEMENT INC. VS. ANGUS GAVIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ***Re-Noticed*** Amended Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default, Default Judgment For Possession, And Judgment For Money Damages; Alternatively, For Relief From Court'S 12/14/2021 Order Denying Defendants' Motion To Set Aside The 10/6/2021 Money Judgment Obtained By Plaintiffs, As Well As The 1/5/2021 Notice Of Entry Of Default And 2 Clerk'S Judgment Dated 1/7/2021.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 4, 2022 line 4. DEFENDANTS ANGUS GAVIN AND JOHN GAVIN'S AMENDED MOTION TO VACATE ENTRY OF DEFAULT, DEFAULT JUDGMENT FOR POSSESSION, AND JUDGMENT FOR MONEY DAMAGES; ALTERNATIVELY, FOR RELIEF FROM COURT'S 12/14/2021 ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE 10/6/2021 MONEY JUDGMENT OBTAINED BY PLAINTIFFS, AS WELL AS THE 1/5/2021 NOTICE OF ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND 2 CLERK'S JUDGMENT DATED 1/7/2021 is GRANTED. The examination of the judgment roll shows no evidence that jurisdiction was properly acquired. Plaintiffs were authorized by Court order to serve the Defendants by posting and mailing by certified mail. The Proof of Service filed on January 5, 2021 attests only to service "by posting and first-class mailing." There is no proof of service on file for certified mail, which is different from first-class mail. The default and default judgment are set aside. Plaintiff may file an amended proof of service as to December 16, 2020 service; or file and serve an amended complaint (it appears that possession is no longer at issue); or file a dismissal no later than February 10, 2022. Defendants shall have 5 days to respond if Plaintiff files an amended proof of service or statutory time to respond if Plaintiff files an amended complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BPREP MOSSO APARTMENTS LLC VS. KAYLA HOLLINGSWORTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION TO PREVENT FORFEITURE DUE TO COVID-19 RENTAL DEBT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 4, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT KAYLA HOLLINGSWORTH'S APPLICATION TO PREVENT FORFEITURE DUE TO COVID-19 RENTAL DEBT Hearing Required. Kayla Hollingsworth to appear in person. At the hearing Ms. Hollingsworth to (1) present the transaction number for e-filing of the document signed on January 24, 2022 and provided to the Sheriff in the morning of January 26, 2022; if Ms. Hollingsworth did not personally submit the document for e-filing Defendant to identify the person or organization that submitted the e-filing, received the filed endorsed copy and provided it to Ms. Hollingsworth; (2) possess log-in information for Housing is Key web-site to log into case number 548295. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: DE LA SALLE INSTITUTE VS. RYAN WHITESELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION TO PREVENT FORFEITURE DUE TO COVID-19 RENTAL DEBT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 4, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT RYAN WHITESELL'S APPLICATION TO PREVENT FORFEITURE DUE TO COVID-19 RENTAL DEBT is DENIED. Defendant failed to establish that there is a final approval for rental assistance for the property, the amount of payment approved and the time period covered. Defendant further failed to show that a copy of the approval is not available. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
